what: Modernized for current R and tidyverse
title: narcan 0.3.0
text: release brings long-dormant package date modern R tidyverse conventions fixes several correctness issues, preserving existing analysis behavior. R CMD check clean (0 errors, 0 warnings).

previous_headings: Modernized for current R and tidyverse
title: narcan 0.3.0
text: Minimum R now 4.2 – package uses native |> pipe internally. flag_suicide_* family gains year argument now warns pre-1999 (ICD-9) data instead silently returning zeros. ICD-9 suicide coding yet implemented; warning fires year determinable < 1999. state_abbrev_to_fips() now zero-pads two-digit FIPS codes (e.g. "06" California, "6"), matching add_county_fips() NCHS convention. unite_records() now errors year outside 1979-1998 / >= 1999 (example two-digit datayear 93) instead silently returning unrelated object. Pass explicit four-digit year.

previous_headings: Modernized for current R and tidyverse
title: narcan 0.3.0
text: main data-consuming functions (unite_records(), flag_drug_deaths(), flag_opioid_deaths(), flag_opioid_types(), flag_od_intent(), calc_asrate_var(), calc_stdrate_var(), add_pop_counts(), add_std_pop()) now emit plain warning first argument data frame missing required column, mistake surfaces name rather cryptic downstream error. checks never abort.

previous_headings: Modernized for current R and tidyverse
title: narcan 0.3.0
text: Dependency calls deprecated defunct current dplyr updated change output: funs() -> across(), mutate_at(vars()) -> mutate(), one_of() -> any_of(), group_by(add = TRUE) -> group_by(.add = TRUE). Converted internal code native |> pipe, modernized rate/summary helpers’ non-standard evaluation {{ }} embracing, replaced tidyr::gather() tidyr::pivot_longer(). Removed unused purrr dependency.

title: narcan 0.2.1
text: recode/label helpers previously hardcoded single coding scheme applied every year, silently mislabeling data years NCHS coding changed. now year-aware. Values verified NCHS public-use mortality file documentation.

title: narcan 0.2.1
text: categorize_hspanicr(hspanicr_column, year = NULL) gains year argument (scalar vector aligned data column). applies 9-category scheme 2020, returns NA reserved 2021 field, applies expanded 14-category (single-race, 1997 OMB) scheme 2022. Values 1989 (recorded) NA. year omitted pre-2022 9-category scheme assumed warning – existing calls keep working pass year label 2022+ data correctly. remap_race(icd_df, year) now dispatches three ways: bridged detailed race 2020 (unchanged), NA 2021 transition gap (bridged race dropped, single-race recodes yet populated), single-race Race Recode 6 (racer5) 2022 mapped non-colliding code space (101-106). internal .remap_race_1992_2015 helper renamed .remap_race_1992_2020. categorize_race() labels single-race codes 101-106 (white_only, black_only, american_indian_only, asian_only, nhopi_only, multiracial) addition bridged codes; factor levels adapt scheme(s) present, pre-2021 output unchanged. Bridged (<=2020) single-race (2022+) race/Hispanic categories comparable; remap_race() categorize_race() warn single-race path used, two must chained single trend.

title: narcan 0.2.1
text: import_mcod_fwf() now guarantees canonical year column (year argument), downstream year dispatch works every era including stacked multi-year data. 1979-1995 files retain original datayear column well. .extract_year() falls back datayear (used 1979-1995 files) year column present.

title: narcan 0.2.1
text: Rates 2021+ single-race deaths need single-race population denominators; add_pop_counts() joins bridged-race pop_est (discontinued NCHS Vintage 2020). See ?add_pop_counts. Single-race denominators future work. New testthat coverage recode functions built small random sample real public MCOD data spanning every coding era.

title: narcan 0.2.0
text: MCOD fixed-width dictionaries re-verified raw NCHS bytes every year (public 1979-2024 restricted 1989-2024), just NBER Stata dictionaries. narcan’s existing positions found highly accurate; release makes small set corrections adds public-use support.

title: narcan 0.2.0
text: mcod_public_fwf_dicts – public-use tier dictionary, import_mcod_fwf(file, year, tier = c("restricted", "public")), single exported importer tiers. public importer keeps restricted-/ suppressed columns (sub-state geography record type 2005, certifier, tobacco, pregnancy) -NA public restricted output column-compatible. Public effective record length year: 440 (1979-2002), 488 (2003-2012), 490 (2013-2019), 817 (2020-2024). add_coded_occupation(df, year) – harmonizes coded occupation/industry across two non-comparable schemes (3-digit Census 1985-1999; 4-digit NCHS+NIOSH 2020+) standard occ_coded/ind_coded/occ_recode/ind_recode columns plus occ_scheme occ_available flag, researchers need know byte positions, era, tier. 4-digit codes reach public file 2020 restricted file 2021 (identical 2021). Coverage extended data years 2023 2024 (tiers). cdc_dict extended latest public-use year (2024); .download_mcod_fwf() now uses CDC HTTPS endpoint (old ftp:// host decommissioned). dictionaries now built reviewed source CSVs (data-raw/fwf_layouts/*.csv) network-free assembly script writes exported internal copies sync.

title: narcan 0.2.0
text: hspanicr widened 1 byte (@488) 2 bytes (@487-488) 2022+. change alters parsed values: reading single byte truncated 14-category Hispanic-origin/race recode ~10 units-digit values (e.g. Mexican 01 collided non-Hispanic Asian 11). parsed 2022+ restricted data narcan < 0.2.0, re-import recover full recode. racer40 (@489-490) now declared restricted 2003-2011 documented--empty field, making declared record length faithful true 490-byte record. adds -NA racer40 column years’ output; values change.
